food_subgroup: Flatbread wrap with cheese and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term for piadina or cheese-filled flatbread wrap. FOODON_00002572 (flatbread food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Flat bread) used as closest parent; no specific piadina or cheese flatbread wrap code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for piadina or cheese flatbread wrap product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Cheesy Melts Piadina wrap, approximately 220 g, consisting of a 10-inch Italian flatbread filled with melted mozzarella and cheddar cheese, olive oil, basil, roasted peppers, and roasted onions.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheese pizza on thin crust; USDA SR Legacy — Mozzarella cheese, part-skim; Cheddar cheese; Olive oil; Flatbread (piadina-type); component-based estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Cheesy Melts Piadina exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values estimated by weighted ingredient composition: piadina flatbread (~55% by weight, USDA SR Legacy plain flatbread), mozzarella and cheddar cheese blend (~30% by weight, USDA SR Legacy part-skim mozzarella and cheddar), olive oil drizzle (~3%), roasted peppers and onions (~12%). Energy and macros cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central cheese pizza thin crust as a plausibility anchor. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this type of cheese-filled flatbread wrap.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Flatbread / piadina-type bread analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Cheesy Melts Piadina exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 based on the closest analogous food: Italian-style flatbread (piadina romagnola, white flour, GI ~52 per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables). The cheese and vegetable filling has minimal independent glycemic contribution. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (52 × 62.7) / 100 ≈ 32.5, where available carbohydrate per 220 g serving = 28.5 × 2.2 = 62.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from cheese blend and flatbread
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1056 mg per full serving), this piadina contributes a substantial portion of the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium target recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and avoidance of additional high-sodium accompaniments is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flatbread driving moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 52 (low) but a glycemic load of approximately 32.5 per full serving due to the large refined-flour flatbread base, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a piadina and pair with non-starchy vegetables or a protein source to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mozzarella and cheddar cheese blend
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The mozzarella–cheddar cheese blend contributes approximately 5.4 g saturated fat per 100 g (approximately 11.9 g per serving), approaching or exceeding the 10–13 g daily saturated fat limit recommended for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with CVD should consume this dish occasionally and in reduced portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Melts Piadina is an Italian-inspired flatbread wrap served at Now Pizza in Singapore. It is built on a 10-inch piadina — a thin, light, and slightly crispy Italian flatbread originating from the Romagna region of Italy — which is filled with a melted blend of mozzarella and cheddar cheese, drizzled with olive oil, and finished with fresh basil leaves, roasted sweet peppers, and roasted onions. The result is a warm, savoury wrap with a tender-crisp exterior, a rich and gooey cheese interior, and a subtle sweetness from the roasted vegetables. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy food at approximately 258 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution from the cheese blend (approximately 10.8 g per 100 g), moderate fat (11.2 g per 100 g, of which 5.4 g is saturated), and a refined-carbohydrate base (28.5 g per 100 g) from the wheat flatbread. Sodium is elevated at approximately 480 mg per 100 g due to the cheese content, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), but the glycemic load per full serving is moderate at approximately 32.5, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is vegetarian but contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is best consumed as a light meal or snack, ideally paired with a salad or non-starchy side to improve nutritional balance.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a piadina wrap?
What cheese is in the Cheesy Melts Piadina?
What is the sodium content of a cheese piadina?
What is the GI of flatbread?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Cheesy Melts Piadina exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted ingredient composition using USDA FoodData Central and USDA SR Legacy data for individual components (flatbread, mozzarella, cheddar, olive oil, roasted vegetables). GI was estimated from the closest analogous food (Italian flatbread, GI ~52) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is systematic and grounded in approved sources, but direct measurement data for this specific commercial product is unavailable.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy or FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. All other micronutrient values are estimated from USDA component data and should be treated as approximate. The serving size of 220 g is estimated based on a standard 10-inch piadina wrap with cheese and vegetable filling; actual restaurant portion may vary. Halal status is listed as false because the restaurant (Now Pizza - Funan) has not been confirmed as a halal-certified establishment in publicly available sources at time of processing. The dish is vegetarian (no meat or seafood). GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on Italian-style flatbread; the cheese and vegetable filling does not materially alter the GI of the flatbread base. Glycemic load per serving = (52 × 62.7) / 100 ≈ 32.5, where 62.7 g is the available carbohydrate in a 220 g serving (28.5 g/100g × 2.2). Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore; Italian romanized name also provided as romanized_local. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes use closest-parent matches as no specific piadina or cheese flatbread wrap code exists in either ontology.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch using USDA FoodData Central and USDA SR Legacy component-based estimation. Documented vitamin_d_mcg = -1 in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency.

Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Final decision set to PASS.
